title: RayleaBot Plugin JSONL Protocol
description: Fixture-ready schema for the RayleaBot platform-runtime JSONL protocol. This contract freezes startup, startup progress, shared outbound segment payloads, the initial OneBot11 wider action family surface, local config/logger/storage/http/scheduler/webhook/render action RPC, event dispatch, result, error, ping/pong keepalive, and shutdown semantics.

sub_type.description: Event sub-type for notice events (e.g., approve, invite, kick, leave).

operator_id.description: The user who triggered the notice event, if different from the actor.

type.description: Segment kind: text, image, at, at_all, face, reply, or implementation-specific types.

raw_payload.description: Only present when the plugin manifest declares event.raw_payload and the corresponding high-sensitivity permission has been granted.

$comment: Frozen plugin action kinds include terminal message.send/message.reply, local-action RPC logger.write/storage.kv/config.read/plugin.list/config.write/storage.file/http.request/scheduler.create/event.expose_webhook/render.image, the initial OneBot11 wider action family, and provider.* namespaces for NapCat and LuckyLillia extensions. init conveys the current bot identity, granted capabilities, and sanitized command_prefixes. message.* uses the shared outbound segment model; local actions use distinct request_ids, may carry parent_request_id to identify the owning event session, and receive result/error frames from the platform before the plugin completes its event. Concurrent plugins must provide parent_request_id on local action frames. event.raw_payload remains a gated high-sensitivity event field rather than a standalone action. Ping/pong remain the minimum keepalive semantics. Batching and complex streaming payloads remain out of scope.
